title: ajuga reptans subvar. densiflora
intro: Ajuga reptans subvar. densiflora, also known as Bugleweed or Ajuga densiflora, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small blue or purple flowers and dark green, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiviral activities, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides such as ajugan and ajugol. The health benefits of Ajuga reptans subvar. densiflora include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and lowering blood pressure, making it a popular remedy for cardiovascular and nervous system disorders. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
